tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting severe renal dysfunction, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria. Low urine specific gravity may indicate impaired concentrating ability. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
